Title: Checkout - Delivery Option Selection
Preconditions: Products in cart
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[ASSERT] Verify shipping options section visible
[ACT] select 'Delivery' option
[WAIT] Wait for address form to render
[ASSERT] Verify shipping address form appears
[ASSERT] Verify shipping method list visible
Assertions:
[DOM] Delivery radio/button is selected state
[DOM] Shipping address form fields rendered (Street City State ZIP Country)
[DOM] At least one shipping method option listed
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET /storefronts/shipping-methods returns non-empty array
[CONSOLE] No errors on delivery option selection
Failure_Signals: Delivery option not selectable; address form does not appear after selection

Title: Checkout - Enter Shipping Address
Preconditions: Delivery selected at checkout, user has no saved addresses
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart and proceed to checkout
[ACT] select 'Delivery' option
[ACT] Fill 'Street' field with valid street address
[ACT] Fill 'City' field with valid city
[ACT] Fill 'State' field with valid state
[ACT] Fill 'ZIP' field with valid postal code
[ACT] Fill 'Country' field with valid country
[ACT] click 'Continue' or 'Save Address'
[ASSERT] Verify address saved and displayed in summary
Assertions:
[DOM] All address fields accept text input
[STATE] Validation passes on all fields (no error states)
[STATE] Address displayed in shipping summary section
[DOM] Continue/Next step button becomes active
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] POST address payload contains all required fields
[CONSOLE] No validation errors in console
Failure_Signals: Form accepts invalid data without error; address not persisted after clicking Continue

Title: Checkout - Select Saved Address

[CONSOLE] No errors when loading saved addresses

Title: Checkout Validation - XSS Prevention in Address Fields
Preconditions: User at checkout shipping address step, cart has at least one item
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to checkout shipping address step [ACT] Enter First Name: '' [ACT] Enter Last Name: 'TestXSS' [ACT] Enter Phone: '' [ACT] Fill Street, City, State, ZIP with valid data [ACT] Click Continue [ASSERT] Verify no XSS alert dialog is triggered (no JavaScript execution) [ACT] Click Continue and wait for form to submit or advance [ASSERT] If checkout advances: navigate to confirmation; verify First Name shown does not execute script (literal text, not rendered HTML) [ASSERT] Verify no alert dialog appeared during or after submission [ACT] Enter Phone: '5551234' [ACT] Click Continue [ASSERT] Verify checkout does not execute embedded script [ACT] Fix all fields: First Name 'John', Phone '555-123-4567' [ACT] Click Continue [ASSERT] Verify form advances with clean data
Assertions:
[DOM] No alert dialog triggered at any point (critical security assertion)
[STATE] No client-side script execution from injected HTML payload
[STATE] Server strips or escapes HTML tags — output shown as literal text (e.g., '<script>' or stripped entirely), NOT rendered/executed HTML
[STATE] Clean data accepted and checkout proceeds after correcting fields
NOTE: No inline client-side validation error is expected for HTML tags — form may accept input and rely on server-side sanitization; this is acceptable behavior. Inline error NOT required.
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No XSS alert dialogs; no uncaught JS errors from injected payload
[API] addOrUpdateCartShipment mutation data does not contain unsanitized executable script
[NETWORK] No 5xx errors on shipment update
Failure_Signals: Alert dialog fires from injected script; HTML renders and executes in name field on confirmation; server returns 500 from unsanitized input
References: VCST-4803 coverage gap — checkout address XSS
Automation_Status: Generated — Rewritten 2026-04-21: removed incorrect assertion [DOM] Error messages shown inline for fields containing HTML tags. Observed behavior: no inline error; server strips/escapes HTML. Assertions updated to structural security invariants (no script execution, server sanitization).

Title: Double Submit Prevention - Button Disabled After First Click
Preconditions: User logged in; all checkout steps complete (address, shipping, payment); user at order review step
Steps:
[NAV] log in to {{FRONT_URL}} and complete all checkout steps to reach order review page
[ASSERT] verify 'Place Order' button is visible and enabled
[ACT] rapidly double-click 'Place Order' button (two clicks within 500ms)
[WAIT] wait for order submission to process
[ASSERT] verify button is disabled after first click (not two submissions fired)
[ASSERT] verify only one order confirmation page or success state reached
Assertions:
[DOM] 'Place Order' button enters disabled/loading state immediately after first click
[STATE] button does not re-enable while submission is in-flight
[STATE] user lands on a single order confirmation page (not two)
[DOM] spinner or loading indicator visible during submission
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] createOrder or changeCartComment mutation called exactly once (check network tab)
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during double-click scenario
[NETWORK] only one POST to order creation endpoint; no duplicate requests
Failure_Signals: button remains active after first click allowing double submission; two confirmation pages displayed; two orders created in backend
Cleanup: Remove duplicate order if created; verify order history shows one entry

Title: Payment Retry After Decline - Retry With Different Card Succeeds
Preconditions: User logged in; all checkout steps complete up to payment; test decline card number available (e.g., Authorize.Net test decline card)

[NAV] log in to {{FRONT_URL}} and proceed to checkout payment step
[ASSERT] verify payment form visible
[ACT] enter invalid or known-decline test card number in card number field
[ACT] fill expiry, CVV, and cardholder name fields
[ACT] click 'Place Order' or 'Pay Now'
[WAIT] wait for decline response
[ASSERT] verify decline message shown (e.g., 'Payment declined. Please try a different card.')
[ASSERT] verify checkout step is still at payment (not redirected away)
[ACT] clear card number field and enter valid test card number
[ACT] click 'Place Order' or 'Pay Now'
[WAIT] wait for order confirmation
Assertions:
[DOM] decline error message visible after first attempt
[STATE] payment form remains on screen (not navigated away from checkout)
[STATE] user can re-enter card details without refreshing the page

Cross_Layer_Checks:

Failure_Signals: page crashes on decline; user redirected to unrecoverable error page; no decline message shown; retry button absent after decline
Cleanup: Remove test order; clear cart if needed

Title: Minimum Order Amount - Checkout Blocked When Below Minimum
Preconditions: ADMIN-CONFIG REQUIRED: Store must have minimum order amount configured in Admin > Stores > General > MinOrderTotal (e.g., $50.00). QA environment does not have this configured by default. Admin step: navigate to Stores > [Store Name] > edit > set MinOrderTotal = 50.00 and save. Then verify {{TEST_SKU}} price is below $50 (e.g., single item < $5).
Steps:
[NAV] log in to {{FRONT_URL}} and add a low-value item to cart (total below minimum order amount)
[NAV] navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[ASSERT] verify cart total is below configured minimum order amount
[ACT] attempt to click 'Proceed to Checkout' or 'Place Order'
[WAIT] wait for validation response
[ASSERT] verify checkout blocked with informational message
[ASSERT] verify message indicates minimum order amount and shortfall (e.g., 'Minimum order is $50.00. Add $45.00 more to proceed.')
Assertions:
[DOM] 'Proceed to Checkout' button disabled or validation message shown
[DOM] message references minimum order amount value
[DOM] message references shortfall amount (amount needed to reach minimum)
[STATE] user cannot advance past cart or checkout steps while below minimum
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] getCart or checkout mutation returns minimum order violation error
[CONSOLE] no JS errors when minimum order block is triggered
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx (validation should be graceful)
Failure_Signals: checkout proceeds despite cart being below minimum; no message shown; vague error message without minimum amount or shortfall; 500 error instead of validation message
Cleanup: Remove test items from cart; reset MinOrderTotal to 0 in Admin
Automation_Status: Manual — BLOCKED: requires admin config (MinOrderTotal) not set in QA. SEED verdict 2026-04-21.

Title: Ship-To Address - Default or Favorite Address Pre-Selected at Checkout
Preconditions: User logged in; user has at least one saved address marked as default or favorite; cart has items
Steps:
[NAV] log in to {{FRONT_URL}} and proceed to checkout
[ACT] select 'Delivery' option if not auto-selected
[WAIT] wait for address section to render
[ASSERT] verify default or favorite address is pre-selected (highlighted or shown as selected)
[ASSERT] verify selected address displays street, city, state, ZIP, and country
[ASSERT] verify 'Proceed' or 'Continue' button active with pre-selected address
Assertions:
[DOM] default/favorite address is selected state at checkout load (no manual selection needed)
[DOM] selected address shows full address fields (street, city, state, ZIP, country)
[STATE] user does not need to manually select an address to proceed
[DOM] Continue/Next step button is active with pre-selected address
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] getCart or checkout query returns pre-selected default address in deliveryAddress field
[CONSOLE] no JS errors on checkout address load
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on address section render
Failure_Signals: no address pre-selected (user must manually pick every time); wrong address pre-selected (not the default/favorite); address form shown blank instead of pre-filled

[NAV] log in to {{FRONT_URL}} and proceed to checkout shipping address step
[ASSERT] verify current address and shipping methods displayed
[ASSERT] note available shipping method count for the pre-selected address
[ACT] click 'Change address' or address search/selector control
[WAIT] wait for address list or search input to appear
[ACT] search for or select a different saved address (different region or country)
[WAIT] wait for checkout to recalculate with new address
[ASSERT] verify selected address updated in checkout summary
[ASSERT] verify shipping methods list refreshed with options for the new address
[ASSERT] verify subtotal and shipping cost updated if applicable
Assertions:
[DOM] new address shown in checkout shipping summary
[STATE] shipping method options updated to reflect new address zone/country
[STATE] previously selected shipping method may reset (valid behavior if incompatible with new address)
[MATH] shipping cost reflects new address zone pricing
Cross_Layer_Checks:

[CONSOLE] no JS errors on address switch and shipping recalculation
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on address change or shipping methods refresh
Failure_Signals: shipping methods not refreshed after address change; old address shown in summary despite selecting new one; shipping cost unchanged after address zone change
Automation_Status: Manual — BLOCKED pending seed: 2+ saved addresses for {{USER_EMAIL}}. SEED verdict 2026-04-21.

Title: Session Timeout During Checkout - Cart Preserved After Re-authentication
Preconditions: TOOL-LIMITATION: Natural session timeout in QA environment is too long for automated testing. Use cookie-clearing approach: MCP browser_evaluate to delete access_token and refresh_token cookies, then proceed. This simulates session expiry without waiting for timeout. NOTE: Real-user-interaction rule applies — use browser_evaluate only for cookie manipulation (not for UI interactions).
Steps:
[NAV] log in to {{FRONT_URL}} and add items to cart; proceed to checkout payment step
[ASSERT] note cart contents (item names, quantities, total)
[ACT] Clear auth cookies via browser DevTools (manually delete access_token/refresh_token cookies) to simulate session expiry
[ACT] attempt to interact with checkout (e.g., click 'Place Order')
[WAIT] wait for response
[ASSERT] verify user redirected to login page or session expired message shown (not a 500 error)
[ACT] log in again with {{USER_EMAIL}} and {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[WAIT] wait for redirect after login
[ASSERT] verify cart contents preserved (same items and quantities as before session expired)
[ASSERT] verify user can resume checkout flow
Assertions:
[DOM] session expiry triggers login redirect or graceful message (not 500)
[STATE] after re-login, cart contains same items and quantities
[STATE] user can proceed through checkout after re-authentication
[DOM] no 'internal server error' or blank page on session expiry
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] requests with expired token return 401 (not 500)
[CONSOLE] no unhandled exceptions on session timeout; 401 handled gracefully
[NETWORK] 401 response on expired session; 200 on successful re-auth
Failure_Signals: 500 error on session expiry; cart emptied after re-login; user redirected to homepage with no message; checkout state lost entirely
Cleanup: Clear test cart items; re-login if needed
Automation_Status: Manual — Rewritten 2026-04-21: natural timeout too long; use cookie-clearing to simulate. TOOL-LIMITATION verdict.

Title: Browser Back Button After Payment - No Duplicate Order Created
Preconditions: User logged in; order successfully placed; user is on order confirmation page
Steps:
[NAV] log in to {{FRONT_URL}} and complete a full checkout to reach the order confirmation page
[ASSERT] note order number from confirmation page
[ACT] press browser back button (or [KEY] Alt+Left arrow)
[WAIT] wait for navigation to resolve (back to review or cart or redirect)
[ASSERT] verify no second order confirmation page rendered with a new order number
[ASSERT] verify appropriate behavior: redirect to cart, or 'Order already placed' message, or checkout steps shown with empty cart
[NAV] navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/account/orders
[ASSERT] verify order history contains exactly one entry matching the noted order number
Assertions:
[STATE] browser back does not trigger a second order submission
[DOM] no second order number generated after pressing back
[DOM] appropriate page shown on back navigation (cart, homepage, or informational message)
[STATE] order history shows exactly one new order from the session
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET /orders shows only one entry for this test session (no duplicate)
[CONSOLE] no JS errors on back navigation after payment
[NETWORK] no second POST to order creation endpoint after back button press
Failure_Signals: second order created after browser back; duplicate order number in order history; blank or error page on back navigation
Cleanup: Remove any duplicate test orders from system

Title: Billing Address Validation - Individual Field Errors Shown
Preconditions: DEPENDENCY: Shipping address and shipping method must be fully selected first (billing section is only visible after). Uncheck 'Same as shipping address' to reveal billing form. See CHK-043 for shipping setup steps.
Steps:
[NAV] log in to {{FRONT_URL}} and proceed to checkout billing address step
[ACT] uncheck 'Same as shipping address' to reveal billing address form
[WAIT] wait for billing address form fields to render
[ACT] leave all billing fields empty (do not fill any)
[ACT] enter an invalid value in 'ZIP' field (e.g., 'ABCDE' for US ZIP)
[ACT] enter an invalid value in 'Phone' field (e.g., 'notaphone')
[ACT] click 'Continue' or 'Place Order' to trigger validation
[WAIT] wait for validation to fire
[ASSERT] verify individual error messages shown inline next to each invalid or empty required field
[ASSERT] verify 'First Name' field shows its own error (not a generic form error)
[ASSERT] verify 'ZIP' field shows its own error message
[ASSERT] verify 'Phone' field shows its own error message
[ASSERT] verify form does not submit while errors present
Assertions:
[DOM] inline validation error shown adjacent to each invalid/empty required field
[DOM] 'First Name' field error visible independently
[DOM] 'ZIP' field error message visible independently
[DOM] 'Phone' field error message visible independently
[STATE] form submission blocked while any required field is invalid
[STATE] correcting a field clears that field's error without clearing other fields
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] no order or billing payload sent while validation errors are present
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during field-level validation
[NETWORK] no outbound POST on failed validation
Failure_Signals: single generic error shown for entire form instead of field-level errors; form submits despite invalid fields; fixing one field clears all others' errors; no validation triggered on submit
Cleanup: Clear test cart; no order placed
Automation_Status: Manual — Rewritten 2026-04-21: added shipping setup dependency (billing form requires shipping complete). BLOCKED in last run due to step ordering.

Title: Billing Address Persists Through Payment Retry
Preconditions: DEPENDENCY: Requires a payment processor that shows a payment form allowing a failed/declined payment attempt (Skyflow, Authorize.Net, or Datatrance — NOT CyberSource which uses cross-origin iframe). Use Manual payment method if available as fallback to test the form state without actual card decline. NOTE: CyberSource iframe is inaccessible to MCP browsers.
Steps:
[NAV] navigate to checkout with items in cart
[ACT] fill shipping address
[ACT] uncheck 'Billing same as shipping' and fill different billing address
[ACT] proceed to payment
[ACT] enter invalid payment details to trigger a decline/failure
[WAIT] wait for payment error message
[ASSERT] verify billing address fields still populated after payment failure
[ASSERT] verify shipping address still populated
[ACT] correct payment details and retry
[ASSERT] verify both addresses still correct on order confirmation
Assertions:
[STATE] billing address not cleared after payment failure
[STATE] shipping address preserved through retry
[DOM] both address forms retain entered values
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] payment retry sends same billing/shipping addresses
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during payment retry
[NETWORK] no 5xx on retry
Failure_Signals: billing address cleared after decline; shipping address lost; user forced to re-enter addresses; addresses swapped
Automation_Status: Manual — Rewritten 2026-04-21: added payment processor dependency (not CyberSource). Use Manual payment or non-iframe processor.

[ACT] select a shipping method

[ASSERT] verify shipping cost displayed and non-negative
[ASSERT] verify tax amount displayed
[ASSERT] verify total = subtotal + shipping + tax - discounts
[ACT] note the exact total amount
Assertions:
[MATH] total = subtotal + shipping + tax - discounts
[MATH] subtotal = sum(unit price x quantity) for each line item
[DOM] all cost components visible in order summary
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] checkout API response totals match displayed values
[CONSOLE] no JS errors
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx
Failure_Signals: total does not equal component sum; missing tax or shipping in calculation; negative total; rounding error > $0.01

Title: Minimum Order Amount - Cart Below Minimum Blocks Checkout
Preconditions: ADMIN-CONFIG REQUIRED: Store must have minimum order amount configured in Admin > Stores > MinOrderTotal (e.g., $50). QA environment does not have this configured by default. Duplicate of CHK-054 scope — these two cases should be consolidated. NOTE: CHK-084 focuses on cart-page block; CHK-054 focuses on checkout-step block.
Steps:
[NAV] navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} and log in
[ACT] add a low-priced product to cart (total below minimum order amount)
[NAV] navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[ASSERT] verify message indicating minimum order amount not met (e.g., 'Minimum order is $50')
[ASSERT] verify checkout/Place Order button is disabled or blocked
[ACT] attempt to proceed to checkout
[ASSERT] verify user cannot advance past cart to checkout
Assertions:
[DOM] minimum order warning message visible
[DOM] checkout button disabled or not clickable
[STATE] checkout flow blocked for below-minimum cart
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] cart validation returns minimum order error
[CONSOLE] no JS errors
[NETWORK] no 5xx
Failure_Signals: checkout proceeds below minimum; no warning message; button enabled despite below-minimum total; generic error without specifying minimum amount
Cleanup: Remove items from cart; reset MinOrderTotal to 0 in Admin
Automation_Status: Manual — BLOCKED: requires admin config (MinOrderTotal) not set in QA. SEED verdict 2026-04-21. See CHK-054 for duplicate scope.

Title: Checkout - Edit Shipping Address Does Not Create Duplicate (VCST-4830)
Preconditions: User logged in with items in cart; shipping address already set during checkout (complete steps up to shipping method selection). Bug: VCST-4830 — editing a shipping address from cart/checkout created a new CartAddress record in DB every time instead of updating the existing one.
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart with items
[ACT] Proceed to checkout
[WAIT] Wait for shipping address step to render
[ACT] Enter first shipping address: First Name="Test", Last Name="User", Street="123 Test St", City="New York", State="NY", ZIP="10001", Country="United States"
[ACT] Click 'Continue' or 'Save Address' to save the shipping address
[WAIT] Wait for shipping methods to appear
[ASSERT] Verify shipping address displayed in summary shows "123 Test St"
[ACT] Click 'Edit' on shipping address section to modify the address
[WAIT] Wait for address form to reopen with pre-filled values
[ASSERT] Verify form is pre-populated with previously entered address ("123 Test St")
[ACT] Change Street field from "123 Test St" to "456 Updated Ave"
[ACT] Click 'Save' or 'Continue' to apply the address update
[WAIT] Wait for address summary to refresh
[ASSERT] Verify shipping address summary now shows "456 Updated Ave" (not "123 Test St")
[ASSERT] Verify shipping methods are still visible and selected method is preserved
[ACT] Click 'Edit' on shipping address section again (second edit)
[WAIT] Wait for address form to reopen
[ASSERT] Verify form shows "456 Updated Ave" (updated value, not original)
[ACT] Change City from "New York" to "Los Angeles", State to "CA", ZIP to "90001"
[ACT] Click 'Save' or 'Continue'
[WAIT] Wait for address summary to refresh
[ASSERT] Verify address summary shows "456 Updated Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90001"
[ASSERT] Verify shipping methods recalculate for new location (may show different rates for CA vs NY)
Assertions:
[DOM] After first edit: address summary shows updated street "456 Updated Ave"
[DOM] After second edit: address summary shows "Los Angeles, CA 90001"
[STATE] Only one shipping address active at a time — no leftover addresses from previous edits
[STATE] Shipping methods recalculate after address region change (NY→CA)
[DOM] No stale address fragments visible anywhere in checkout UI
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[GQL] addOrUpdateCartShipment mutation returns 200, errors[] empty on each edit
[GQL] After each edit, query cart → shipments count = 1 (not growing with each edit)
[GQL] cart.shipments[0].deliveryAddress reflects latest values
[NETWORK] No duplicate addOrUpdateCartShipment calls per single edit action
[CONSOLE] No JS errors during address editing
Failure_Signals: Shipping address summary still shows old address after edit; address form not pre-populated on re-edit; shipping methods don't recalculate after address change; multiple addOrUpdateCartShipment calls per edit (potential duplicate); checkout breaks after editing address multiple times
Automation_Status: Manual — Rewritten 2026-04-21: GQL cross-layer check added (cart.shipments count must = 1 after each edit). BLOCKED in REG-2026-04-20 run due to VCST-4830 fix verification pending; verify fix is deployed before running.
